persona.name: Sophia
persona.attributes: 22 years old. Part-time Barista and College Student. Third trimester. Unplanned pregnancy. Worried about finishing her education while caring for a newborn.

transcript.content: To make sure the support a health institution offers actually fits your life right now, could you walk me through what a “typical week” looks like for you in this third trimester—between classes, work shifts, appointments, and rest—and where it feels most difficult to keep everything balanced?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

Thanks for sharing that you’re feeling overwhelmed — that makes a lot of sense with so many moving parts right now.

When during the week do you notice the overwhelm peak most (for example: right after a long shift, on clinic/appointment days, at night when you’re trying to study), and what’s usually happening in that moment?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

When that overwhelmed feeling peaks, what do you find yourself needing most in that exact moment—practical help (like scheduling/transport), emotional reassurance, or clear information—and what’s the hardest part about getting that need met right now?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

When that overwhelmed feeling hits, it’s usually at night—especially after I’ve been on my feet at work and then I’m trying to switch into “student mode.” I’ll sit down to study and my body just feels done, but my brain is racing about everything I still have to do before the baby comes.

In that exact moment, I think I need two things, but the biggest is practical help—like someone helping me map out what actually has to happen and when. Not even big life advice, just: “Here are the next steps, here are the deadlines, here’s how to schedule your appointments around classes, here’s what you can postpone.” It feels like everything is urgent and I can’t tell what matters most.

The hardest part is I don’t know who to ask, and I don’t have time to chase answers. Like, if I call a clinic or a school office, I get put on hold, or they tell me to call somewhere else, and then I give up because I’m exhausted. And I also feel kind of embarrassed, because the pregnancy wasn’t planned and I don’t want to be judged or treated like I was irresponsible. So I end up trying to figure it out alone.
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

That makes a lot of sense—especially the “everything feels urgent” part, and how being bounced between offices would be exhausting even on a good day.

When you imagine the most helpful kind of practical support in that nighttime moment, what format would actually work for you: a single point-person you can text/call, a short step-by-step checklist with timelines, or a scheduled planning session with someone—and what would make you feel *not judged* while getting that help?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
